# CDE/PII Tagging: silver-base-bea-rpp

**Table:** `silver.base.bea_rpp` (Iceberg: `base.bea_rpp`)
**Spec:** `docs/specs/silver-base-bea-rpp.md`
**Parent Spec:** `docs/specs/raw-ingest-bea-rpp.md`
**Date:** 2026-04-10
**Agent:** @cde-tagger
**Zone:** Silver (normalize + model)
**Contract:** `governance/data-contracts/silver-base-bea-rpp.yaml` *(to be generated by @doc-generator — embed the flags below)*
**Upstream tagging:** `governance/cde-tagging/raw-ingest-bea-rpp.md` (Bronze, 4 CDEs, 0 PII)

---

## Domain Context Referenced

- `governance/domain-context.md` — BEA Regional Price Parities section (lines 1352–1620)
- `governance/domain-context.md` §BEA RPP §Source Codes → Business Concepts (lines 1522–1529) — **explicit @cde-tagger guidance:**
  - `rpp_all_items` → auto-approve as CDE, cross-reference BT-098
  - `geo_fips` / `state_fips` → auto-approve as CDE (ANSI/FIPS standard)
  - `geo_name` / `state_name` → auto-approve
  - `purchasing_power_multiplier` (Silver) → FutureProof-derived, cross-reference BT-099
- `governance/domain-context.md` §BEA RPP §Canonical Concept Map (lines 1535–1549) — lists State Identifier, Purchasing Power Multiplier, Data Year, and Census Region as CORE business concepts; only Census Region flagged EXTENDED
- `governance/pii-scans/silver-base-bea-rpp.md` — delta scan decision: **NO PII** across all 11 Silver columns. Grain unchanged at 51 state rows, k-anonymity floor ~584,000 (Wyoming). Zero-PII claim from Bronze holds across all Silver transformations.
- `governance/eda/silver-base-bea-rpp.md` (via audit trail) — 51 rows, 11 columns, four census regions all present, verification_status enum `{bea_official, estimate}` with 8 `bea_official` / 43 `estimate`
- **Applicable regulations:** NONE — BEA RPP is U.S. Government-published aggregate economic statistics in the public domain. No HIPAA, FERPA, GLBA, SOX, GDPR, CCPA, or PCI DSS exposure at this zone.
- **PII expectations:** NONE — all 11 Silver columns are either state-level aggregates, deterministic transformations of state FIPS, or pipeline metadata.

---

## Table Context: Why CDE Decisions Matter Here

`silver.base.bea_rpp` is a **51-row state reference table** with 11 columns — four Bronze passthrough / rename fields, five newly derived business fields, and three pipeline metadata fields. Its function in the pipeline is to be the **canonical Silver state reference** that the Gold consumable (`consumable.regional_price_parities`), the MCP tools (`get_regional_price_parity`, `compare_purchasing_power`), and ultimately every salary figure displayed to any student consume. The Silver transformation adds three user-facing capabilities that did not exist in Bronze:

1. **User-friendly state identification** via `state_abbr` — the primary handle the frontend and MCP tool parameters use
2. **Pre-computed purchasing power math** via `purchasing_power_multiplier` — every downstream adjustment is a single multiply against this factor
3. **Per-row provenance hedging** via `verification_status` — so Gemma can soften numeric precision on the 43 estimated rows vs. the 8 BEA-verified rows

Each of these is load-bearing for a downstream consumer. They are newly evaluated as CDEs in this zone, independent of the Bronze flags (per Brightsmith no-backward-propagation policy).

Per Brightsmith governance policy, CDE flags do **not** propagate across zones. This tagging covers only Silver columns. Gold will be tagged on its own contract when `consumable.regional_price_parities` is generated.

---

## Columns Flagged as CDE

| # | Column | Type | is_cde | Source | Rationale |
|---|--------|------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | `state_fips` | string | **true** | Bronze passthrough (renamed from `geo_fips`) | **Primary key** of the Silver table, **dedup grain** for `compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpp')`, and the join anchor that every downstream zone (Gold, MCP tools, frontend state selector) resolves back to. No other column can substitute: `state_name` is a display label, `state_abbr` is 1:1-isomorphic-but-derived, `rpp_all_items` is not unique (Iowa and Oklahoma tie at 87.8). Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Domain-context.md §BEA RPP auto-approves as CDE. ANSI/FIPS external standard. |
| 2 | `state_name` | string | **true** | Bronze passthrough (renamed from `geo_name`) | Full human-readable state name. Flagged because it is the **primary display label** served by MCP tool responses (`"state_name": "California"`) and by the frontend salary adjustment UI ("In California, that's equivalent to about $58,700 in purchasing power"). Also the fallback identifier when a user or Gemma passes state by full name rather than FIPS or abbreviation — the MCP tool `get_regional_price_parity(state)` accepts all three. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Domain-context.md §BEA RPP auto-approves as CDE. |
| 3 | `state_abbr` | string | **true** | **New in Silver** — derived from `state_fips` via static 51-entry USPS lookup | **The identifier the frontend uses.** The student's state selector widget stores a 2-letter USPS code; the MCP tool's `state` parameter accepts it; every chart/display label shows it; every URL and query string uses it. Without this column, the frontend cannot round-trip the user's state selection back to this row. It is 1:1 with `state_fips`, but `state_fips` is a machine code — `state_abbr` is the human-readable key the entire product surface area is built on. BT-RPP-STATE-ABBR (proposed; part of the State Identifier business concept per domain-context.md §Canonical Concept Map). **New CDE flag vs. Bronze.** |
| 4 | `census_region` | string | **true** | **New in Silver** — derived from `state_fips` via static FIPS→region lookup | Four-valued Census Bureau enum (`Northeast`, `Midwest`, `South`, `West`). Used by the frontend for **regional comparison views** (e.g., "how does this career play out across the South?") and for the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss's regional cost tiering. The spec's Silver DQ rules P0-enforce both value domain (`IN ('Northeast','Midwest','South','West')`) and completeness (all four represented), which indicates it is business-critical enough to guard. Not a join key — the join remains `state_fips` / `state_abbr` — but it is a decision input to downstream regional roll-ups and boss-fight scoring. Marginal vs. clear CDE; flagged **true** because (a) the spec authors put P0 DQ coverage on it, (b) it is a newly derived business-semantic field, (c) it has no substitute (no other column in this table encodes region), and (d) consumers downstream already plan to use it. **New CDE flag vs. Bronze.** |
| 5 | `rpp_all_items` | double | **true** | Bronze passthrough, unchanged | **The entire analytical payload of this table.** Every derived field in Silver (`purchasing_power_multiplier`) and every Gold transformation (`cost_tier`, `adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k`) and every user-facing salary adjustment is a pure function of this column. If this value is wrong for a state, every salary figure displayed to every student who selects that state is wrong. BT-098 (Regional Price Parity). Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Domain-context.md §BEA RPP auto-approves as CDE. **Highest-criticality column in the table.** |
| 6 | `purchasing_power_multiplier` | double | **true** | **New in Silver** — computed as `100.0 / rpp_all_items` | **Pre-computed salary adjustment factor** consumed directly by Gemma's MCP tool response, by the Gold zone (`consumable.regional_price_parities`), and by the frontend salary displays. Every `adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k` field in Gold is just `N × purchasing_power_multiplier`; every ad-hoc salary adjustment Gemma narrates ("$65,000 in California ≈ $58,722 purchasing power") is a single multiply against this factor. It is mathematically a pure function of `rpp_all_items` (could be derived on the fly), but it is materialized here so (a) Gold, MCP, and frontend don't have to re-derive and (b) the inverse-invariant DQ rule (`purchasing_power_multiplier × rpp_all_items ≈ 100.0`) can guard computation correctness at rest. BT-099 (Purchasing Power Multiplier). Domain-context.md §BEA RPP explicitly calls it out as a FutureProof-derived term to cross-reference BT-099. **New CDE flag vs. Bronze.** |
| 7 | `verification_status` | string | **true** | **New in Silver** — derived from 8-state allow-list of BEA-verified FIPS codes | **Per-row provenance that Gemma uses to hedge numeric precision.** Two-valued enum: `bea_official` (8 rows — California, Hawaii, DC, New Jersey, Arkansas, Mississippi, Iowa, Oklahoma — from the February 2026 BEA release) or `estimate` (43 rows — primary-agent plausible placeholders until the live BEA API load succeeds). This column is **consumed by the MCP tool response** and governs whether Gemma says "California's RPP is 110.7" vs. "California's RPP is approximately 108". It is also a **direct input to the data contract's quality tier** (per domain-context.md: "High for the 8 verified rows / Medium for the 43 estimated rows"). Without this flag, downstream consumers cannot distinguish authoritative values from placeholders, which is a material correctness concern. BT-RPP-VERIFICATION-STATUS (proposed, project-specific). **New CDE flag vs. Bronze** — this field does not exist in Bronze. |
| 8 | `data_year` | int | **true** | Bronze passthrough, unchanged | Provenance-critical temporal dimension. RPP index is re-published annually by BEA; values shift year-over-year as the underlying price index methodology updates. A mis-labeled `data_year` silently stales every downstream purchasing-power figure. Governs annual refresh replacement semantics — when the 2025 RPPs publish, this column is the switch that tells the pipeline the row is eligible for replacement. Constant `2024` in current load. P0 DQ rule pins `data_year = 2024`. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. |

**Total CDE flags: 8 of 11 columns.**

Breakdown:
- **4 carried forward from Bronze:** `state_fips`, `state_name`, `rpp_all_items`, `data_year`
- **4 new Silver CDEs:** `state_abbr`, `census_region`, `purchasing_power_multiplier`, `verification_status`

---

## Columns Flagged as PII

**None.** All 11 Silver columns are non-PII. Per `governance/pii-scans/silver-base-bea-rpp.md`:

- `state_fips`, `state_name`, `state_abbr`, `census_region` are public jurisdiction identifiers, categorically non-PII under HIPAA Safe Harbor §164.514(b)(2)(i)(B) (geographic units of first-three-ZIP-digits-or-larger are safe; a state is far larger; a census region is larger still).
- `rpp_all_items` and `purchasing_power_multiplier` are macroeconomic aggregates / deterministic transformations of one — not individual measurements.
- `verification_status` is a provenance enum over state-level aggregates, not individuals.
- `record_id` is a deterministic hash of `state_fips` — a pseudonym for a jurisdiction, not a person.
- `data_year`, `source_load_date`, `ingested_at` are pipeline / publication timing, not personal dates.
- k-anonymity floor is unchanged from Bronze at ~584,000 (Wyoming population). Every row represents hundreds of thousands to tens of millions of people in aggregate.

No column receives `is_pii: true`. No sensitivity classification above `public` is required. No RLS, no column masking, no encryption-beyond-baseline.

---

## Columns Evaluated — Not Flagged

| # | Column | Type | is_cde | is_pii | Reason Not Critical / Not Sensitive |
|---|--------|------|--------|--------|-------------------------------------|
| 9 | `record_id` | string | false | false | Deterministic Silver grain identifier built as `compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpp')`. Pure function of `state_fips`, so it encodes no additional information — flagging both would be redundant. Consumed only by the pipeline's dedup / upsert machinery, not by business consumers, MCP tools, or user-facing displays. Not a decision input. |
| 10 | `source_load_date` | date | false | false | Bronze passthrough (renamed from `load_date`). Date the load ran. Identical across all 51 rows. Pipeline freshness metadata only. Not a decision input. Not a personal date. |
| 11 | `ingested_at` | timestamp | false | false | Silver promotion timestamp. Identical across all 51 rows in a batch. Useful for operational observability and freshness tracking but not a decision input to any downstream product. Freshness is governed by annual refresh cadence, not per-row timestamp. |

---

## Tag List for Data Contract (for @doc-generator to embed)

When `governance/data-contracts/silver-base-bea-rpp.yaml` is generated, embed the following column-level flags:

```yaml
# Fragment — to be merged by @doc-generator into the silver-base-bea-rpp.yaml contract
columns:
  - name: record_id
    type: string
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Deterministic Silver grain identifier. Derived as compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpp'). 51 distinct values, one per state."

  - name: state_fips
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-RPP-STATE-FIPS  # or the canonical State Identifier term per glossary
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Primary key of the Silver table and dedup grain for compute_grain_id(['state_fips'], prefix='rpp'). The join anchor every downstream zone (Gold, MCP tools, frontend state selector) resolves back to. ANSI/FIPS external standard. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "2-digit zero-padded ANSI/FIPS state code (e.g., '06' California, '19' Iowa, '11' DC)."

  - name: state_name
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-RPP-STATE-NAME  # or canonical State Identifier term per glossary
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Primary display label served by MCP tool responses and frontend salary-adjustment UI. Fallback identifier when user/LLM passes state by full name rather than FIPS or abbreviation. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Full human-readable U.S. state or jurisdiction name (e.g., 'California', 'Iowa', 'District of Columbia')."

  - name: state_abbr
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-RPP-STATE-ABBR  # proposed; part of State Identifier concept
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "The identifier the frontend uses for state display, the MCP tool parameter, and the user's state selector. Primary human-readable key round-tripped from the UI back to this row. Derived 1:1 from state_fips but functionally load-bearing — the product surface area is built on it. Newly flagged in Silver (does not exist in Bronze)."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "2-letter USPS state/jurisdiction abbreviation (e.g., 'CA', 'IA', 'DC'). Derived from state_fips via static 51-entry lookup; DQ rule enforces 2-character uppercase."

  - name: census_region
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-RPP-CENSUS-REGION  # proposed, project-specific
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Four-valued U.S. Census Bureau regional grouping consumed by frontend regional comparison views and the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss. Spec P0-enforces both value domain and completeness (all four regions present). No substitute column encodes region. Newly flagged in Silver (does not exist in Bronze)."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "U.S. Census Bureau region of the state: one of 'Northeast', 'Midwest', 'South', 'West'. Derived from state_fips via static lookup. DC is assigned to 'South' per Census classification."

  - name: rpp_all_items
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-098
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "The entire analytical payload of this table. Every derived field in Silver (purchasing_power_multiplier) and Gold (cost_tier, adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k) is a pure function of this column. Wrong values here mis-state every salary adjustment displayed to any student who selects that state. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "BEA Regional Price Parity index, All Items line code, national baseline = 100.0. Observed range in current load: [86.9, 110.7]."

  - name: purchasing_power_multiplier
    type: double
    required: true
    business_term: BT-099
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Pre-computed salary adjustment factor (100.0 / rpp_all_items) consumed directly by Gemma's MCP tool response, the Gold zone, and the frontend salary displays. Every downstream salary adjustment is a single multiply against this factor. Materialized at rest so the inverse-invariant DQ rule (multiplier * rpp_all_items ~ 100.0) can guard computation correctness. Newly flagged in Silver (does not exist in Bronze)."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Factor by which a national salary is multiplied to express local purchasing power. Derived as 100.0 / rpp_all_items. DQ range: 0.7 <= x <= 1.3."

  - name: verification_status
    type: string
    required: true
    business_term: BT-RPP-VERIFICATION-STATUS  # proposed, project-specific
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Per-row provenance that Gemma uses to hedge numeric precision in MCP tool responses (bea_official vs. estimate). Direct input to the data contract's quality tier — 8 bea_official rows are High tier, 43 estimate rows are Medium tier per domain-context.md. Without this flag downstream consumers cannot distinguish authoritative BEA values from primary-agent placeholder values. Newly flagged in Silver (does not exist in Bronze)."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Row-level provenance enum: 'bea_official' (8 rows from the February 2026 BEA release) or 'estimate' (43 rows, primary-agent plausible placeholders pending live BEA API load)."

  - name: data_year
    type: int
    required: true
    business_term: BT-RPP-DATA-YEAR
    is_cde: true
    cde_rationale: "Provenance-critical temporal dimension. RPP index is re-published annually; mis-labeled vintage silently stales every downstream purchasing-power figure. Governs annual-refresh replacement semantics. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set."
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Year of the RPP estimate. Constant 2024 across all 51 rows in the current load."

  - name: source_load_date
    type: date
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Date the Bronze load ran. Operational freshness metadata. Identical across all 51 rows in a batch."

  - name: ingested_at
    type: timestamp
    required: true
    is_cde: false
    cde_rationale: ""
    is_pii: false
    pii_rationale: ""
    description: "Silver promotion timestamp. Identical across all 51 rows in a batch."
```

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Columns evaluated | 11 |
| Columns flagged CDE | **8** (`state_fips`, `state_name`, `state_abbr`, `census_region`, `rpp_all_items`, `purchasing_power_multiplier`, `verification_status`, `data_year`) |
| Columns carried forward from Bronze CDE set | 4 (`state_fips`, `state_name`, `rpp_all_items`, `data_year`) |
| New Silver CDE flags | 4 (`state_abbr`, `census_region`, `purchasing_power_multiplier`, `verification_status`) |
| Columns flagged PII | **0** |
| Columns not flagged | 3 (`record_id`, `source_load_date`, `ingested_at` — pipeline metadata) |
| Regulatory frameworks triggered | None |
| Sensitivity classification | `public` across all 11 columns |

**Downstream reminder:** CDE flags do not propagate. When @cde-tagger runs on `gold-regional-price-parities`, it will re-evaluate each derived column (`cost_tier`, `adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k`) on its own merits in the Gold zone.
